items.name: Raw mackerel
items.examine: Striped like sheet music. It surfaced on the downbeat.

items.name: Mackerel
items.examine: Oily, honest, and cooked through. Holds a low note nicely.

items.name: Raw pike
items.examine: All teeth and opinions. Took the bait out of spite.

items.name: Pike
items.examine: Cooked pike. Bonier than an argument, twice as filling.

items.name: Raw bass
items.examine: It provides the bottom end of the shoal's harmony. Provided.

items.name: Bass
items.examine: Cooked bass. The Galley insists the pun is intentional.

items.name: Raw tunaling
items.examine: A young tuna that wandered into the wrong verse.

items.name: Tunaling
items.examine: Seared tunaling. Tender enough to apologise to.

items.name: Raw chimefin
items.examine: Its fins ring faintly when the Tide-Bell sounds. It answered. That was its mistake.

items.name: Chimefin
items.examine: Cooked chimefin. It still hums, very quietly, in B-flat.

items.name: Bronze-ringed net
items.examine: A small net weighted with bronze rings. The shoal finds it persuasive.

items.name: Weighted seine
items.examine: A serious net for serious fish. Mackerel sign no waivers.

items.name: Feather rod
items.examine: Whips like a conductor's baton. Pike rise to the downstroke.

items.name: Resonant rod
items.examine: The line hums a lure-note. Fish call it unsporting.

items.name: Iron harpoon
items.examine: Heavier, sharper, and far less negotiable than the standard model.

items.name: Tidesong harpoon
items.examine: Barbed brass, tuned to the Tide-Bell. The only thing a chimefin respects.

items.name: Wicker cage
items.examine: Woven tight enough to disappoint a bass.

items.name: Brass cage
items.examine: A cage that rings shut. Crustaceans check the reviews too late.

items.name: Tide-Token
items.examine: Harbour scrip stamped with a wave. Sella's ledger says it's worth exactly one favour.

items.name: Onion
items.examine: It will make you cry. The Galley calls this 'seasoning the cook'.

items.name: Chopped onion
items.examine: Diced fine. The tears were a fixed cost.

items.name: Bowl of water
items.examine: Fresh from the Galley's rain butt. Soup waiting for a reason.

items.name: Pinch of sea salt
items.examine: Scooped from the pans. The sea, reduced to its argument.

items.name: Fish stew
items.examine: Bass, onion, and patience in a bowl. The Hearthchoir's steadiest chord.

items.name: Seasoned shark
items.examine: Shark, salted until it stopped holding a grudge.

items.name: Galley toque
items.examine: A tall white hat. Height of hat, the cooks insist, equals steadiness of pitch.

items.name: Galley apron
items.examine: Scorched in places that tell stories. Hearthchoir issue.

items.name: Galley mitts
items.examine: Thick mitts for gripping pans and reputations.

items.name: Oilskin hat
items.examine: Keeps the rain out and the gull commentary muffled.

items.name: Oilskin coat
items.examine: Waterproof, windproof, and nearly excuse-proof.

items.name: Oilskin waders
items.examine: Wade in up to the chorus. The full oilskin set keeps your line luckier.

items.name: Tideturner's cape
items.examine: Sea-silk that ripples on its own tide. Worn at the throat by those the harbour sings about.

objs.name: Shoal fishing spot
objs.examine: The Shoalrings boil with small silver. They surface on the swell, right on cue.

objs.name: Deep-bait fishing spot
objs.examine: Darker water with an appetite. Bring bait and a sturdy rod.

objs.name: Reef cage spot
objs.examine: Brinemaw's ledges crawl with armoured tenants behind on rent.

objs.name: Deep harpoon spot
objs.examine: Big shadows patrol the reef drop-off. Some of them patrol back.

objs.name: Resonant fishing spot
objs.examine: Still water that listens. When the Tide-Bell rings, something answers.

objs.name: Bell lever
objs.examine: A brass lever, polished by hopeful hands. Pull it and the harbour holds its breath.

objs.name: The Tide-Bell
objs.examine: The great brass bell of Saltsong. It calls the catch; the catch, regrettably, comes.

objs.name: Salt pan
objs.examine: Shallow stone trays where the sea is slowly talked out of its water.

npcs.name: Harbourmaster Sella
npcs.examine: Runs Saltsong by tide-table and glare. Pays in Tide-Tokens, never in compliments.

npcs.name: Tackle-Keeper Orin
npcs.examine: Knows every knot and most of the fish by grudge.

npcs.name: Galley-Cook Maren
npcs.examine: Conducts six ranges at once. None of them dares burn.

npcs.name: Fishmonger Pelline
npcs.examine: Pell of Brackwater's sister. She got the better harbour and never lets him forget it.

npcs.name: Lighthouse-Keeper Brann
npcs.examine: Keeps the light, the bell, and several decades of weather in his beard.

sharedEdits.what: Register the Saltsong intent domains (side-effect import, next to the existing './intent-produce' import around line 40). Without it the server never registers salt-fish/salt-bell/salt-scoop/salt-prep/salt-exchange/salt-redeem.
sharedEdits.snippet: import './intent-saltsong'; // side-effect: registers the Saltsong Harbour fishing/cooking/token domains

sharedEdits.what: Load the Saltsong client pack (side-effect import, append with the other pack imports).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './saltsong';            // Saltsong Harbour fishing & cooking expansion
